TITLE: build: pyproject.toml migration + dependency reconciliation
LABELS: roadmap, type:build, size:S, phase:A-foundation
---
## Context

Packaging still lives in a legacy `setup.py` with no `pyproject.toml`, and the dependency story has drifted into three inconsistencies: (1) `imagecodecs` is declared in `setup.py` but missing from `requirements.txt`, so a `pip install -r requirements.txt` environment cannot open LZW-compressed TIFFs; (2) the ultralytics version is stated three different ways across the repo; (3) `requirements.txt` mixes runtime and dev dependencies, and CI installs `pytest-xvfb` ad hoc without it being declared anywhere. Migrating to a PEP 621 `pyproject.toml` gives one source of truth, a proper `dev` extra, and modern-tooling compatibility.

## Current state

- `setup.py` (repo root, 64 lines) — the only packaging metadata. Runtime deps at lines 35–57: `PyQt6>=6.7.0`, `numpy>=2.0.0`, `Pillow>=10.0.0`, `tifffile>=2023.0.0`, `imagecodecs>=2023.1.23` (line 40, with the `#56` comment), `czifile>=2019.7.2`, `opencv-python>=4.8.0`, `pyyaml>=6.0.0`, `scikit-image>=0.21.0`, `ultralytics>=8.0.0` (line 45), `plotly>=5.0.0`, `shapely>=2.0.0`, `pystackreg>=0.2.7`, `pydicom>=2.3.0`, `torch>=2.2.0`, `torchvision>=0.17.0`, `transformers>=4.40.0`, `huggingface_hub>=0.20.0`, `mlflow>=2.0.0`. Entry points at lines 58–63: `digitalsreeni-image-annotator` and `sreeni`, both → `digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main:main`. Version `"0.9.0"` at line 13; `python_requires=">=3.10"` (line 34); classifiers for 3.10–3.14 (lines 28–32); src layout via `find_packages(where="src")` + `package_dir={"": "src"}` (lines 20–21); upstream URL at line 19.
- `requirements.txt` (repo root, 31 lines) — duplicates the runtime list **without `imagecodecs`** (discrepancy), and appends dev deps at lines 27–31: `pytest>=8.0.0`, `pytest-qt>=4.4.0`, `pytest-cov>=4.1.0`, `pytest-mock>=3.12.0`. No `pytest-xvfb`, although CI installs it.
- Ultralytics version stated three inconsistent ways:
  - `CLAUDE.md` "Tech Stack": "Ultralytics 8.3.27 (SAM 2)"
  - `setup.py:45`: `ultralytics>=8.0.0`
  - `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/training/sam_trainer.py:7`: "…``SAM(...).train()`` raises ``NotImplementedError`` (verified on ultralytics 8.4.51)"
- `.github/workflows/tests.yml`:
  - line 43: pip cache key `${{ hashFiles('requirements.txt') }}`
  - lines 47–51: `pip install -e .` then `pip install pytest pytest-qt pytest-cov pytest-mock pytest-xvfb`
- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/__init__.py:10`: `__version__ = "0.9.0"`. The package import is deliberately Qt-free (lazy `__getattr__`, lines 13–44), so a build-time `attr:` version read is safe.
- `pytest.ini` (repo root) holds pytest config — it works and is **out of scope** to move.
- No `MANIFEST.in`, no `setup.cfg` (verified: repo root contains only `setup.py` + `requirements.txt` for packaging).
- `README.md` Development section (lines 224–228) documents `pip install -e .`.

## Proposed design

Replace `setup.py` with a PEP 621 `pyproject.toml` (setuptools backend, src layout). Delete `requirements.txt` (CI and docs point at the package extras instead — one source of truth). Pick `ultralytics>=8.3.27,<9` as the reconciled bound (lowest version the code is documented against, capped below the next major; 8.4.51 remains satisfiable). Version stays single-sourced from `__init__.py` via `dynamic = ["version"]`.

Full proposed `pyproject.toml`:

```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "digitalsreeni-image-annotator"
dynamic = ["version"]
description = "A tool for annotating images using manual and automated tools, supporting multi-dimensional images and SAM2-assisted annotations"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
license = { text = "MIT" }
authors = [
    { name = "Dr. Sreenivas Bhattiprolu", email = "digitalsreeni@gmail.com" },
]
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 3 - Alpha",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
]
dependencies = [
    "PyQt6>=6.7.0",
    "numpy>=2.0.0",           # pip resolves 2.4+ on Py3.14, 2.2.x on Py3.10
    "Pillow>=10.0.0",
    "tifffile>=2023.0.0",
    "imagecodecs>=2023.1.23", # tifffile needs it for LZW/compressed TIFF (#56)
    "czifile>=2019.7.2",
    "opencv-python>=4.8.0",
    "pyyaml>=6.0.0",
    "scikit-image>=0.21.0",
    "ultralytics>=8.3.27,<9", # SAM 2 integration; trainer verified on 8.4.51
    "plotly>=5.0.0",
    "shapely>=2.0.0",
    "pystackreg>=0.2.7",
    "pydicom>=2.3.0",
    "torch>=2.2.0",           # first release with NumPy 2.x compatibility
    "torchvision>=0.17.0",
    "transformers>=4.40.0",
    "huggingface_hub>=0.20.0",
    "mlflow>=2.0.0",          # experiment tracking (#74); always-on core dep
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-qt>=4.4.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.1.0",
    "pytest-mock>=3.12.0",
    "pytest-xvfb>=3.0.0; sys_platform == 'linux'",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/cofade/digitalsreeni-image-annotator"
Upstream = "https://github.com/bnsreenu/digitalsreeni-image-annotator"

[project.scripts]
digitalsreeni-image-annotator = "digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main:main"
sreeni = "digitalsreeni_image_annotator.main:main"

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["src"]

[tool.setuptools.dynamic]
version = { attr = "digitalsreeni_image_annotator.__version__" }
```

CI (`.github/workflows/tests.yml`) changes:
- line 43: cache key → `${{ hashFiles('pyproject.toml') }}` (restore-keys unchanged).
- lines 47–51: replace the two install lines with `pip install -e ".[dev]"` (quotes matter on zsh/pwsh runners).

## Implementation steps

1. Create branch `feature/pyproject-migration`.
2. Add `pyproject.toml` exactly as above.
3. Delete `setup.py` and `requirements.txt` (`git rm`).
4. Update `.github/workflows/tests.yml` (cache key + install step, as specified above).
5. Update `CLAUDE.md` "Tech Stack" line to the reconciled statement, e.g. `Ultralytics >=8.3.27,<9 (SAM 2)`. Leave the `sam_trainer.py:7` docstring alone — "verified on 8.4.51" is a factual claim about a verification run, not a version pin.
6. Update `README.md` Development section (lines 224–228): dev installs become `pip install -e ".[dev]"`; keep the plain `pip install -e .` mention for runtime-only. Touch nothing else in README (a separate docs issue rewrites it — keep this diff minimal to avoid conflicts).
7. Grep for stragglers: `grep -rn "requirements.txt\|setup.py" --include="*.md" --include="*.yml" .` and fix every reference (e.g. `TESTING.md` if it mentions `requirements.txt`).
8. Verify locally, in a fresh venv:
   - `pip install -e ".[dev]"` succeeds.
   - `python -c "import digitalsreeni_image_annotator as m; print(m.__version__)"` prints `0.9.0`.
   - `pip show digitalsreeni-image-annotator` shows version `0.9.0` (dynamic version resolved).
   - `digitalsreeni-image-annotator --help` or a 3-second headless launch (`QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen timeout 3 digitalsreeni-image-annotator`; exit code 124 = launched fine) and the same for `sreeni`.
   - `python -m build --sdist --wheel` (install `build` ad hoc) completes; inspect the wheel contains `digitalsreeni_image_annotator/` and not `tests/`.
9. Run the test suite and smoke tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pyproject.toml` exists with PEP 621 metadata; `setup.py` and `requirements.txt` are deleted.
- [ ] Both console scripts `digitalsreeni-image-annotator` and `sreeni` install and launch (headless check above).
- [ ] `imagecodecs>=2023.1.23` is present in the single dependency list.
- [ ] Ultralytics is pinned once as `>=8.3.27,<9`, and `CLAUDE.md` Tech Stack matches it.
- [ ] `pip install -e ".[dev]"` installs pytest, pytest-qt, pytest-cov, pytest-mock (and pytest-xvfb on Linux); no dev tool remains in the runtime `dependencies`.
- [ ] Version is single-sourced: only `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/__init__.py:10` states `0.9.0`; `pip show` reports it.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/tests.yml` installs via the `dev` extra and its cache key hashes `pyproject.toml`; CI is green on all matrix entries.
- [ ] `grep -rn "requirements.txt" .github docs *.md` returns no live references (historical mentions in release notes are fine).
- [ ] `pytest tests/ -v` and `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` pass.

## Test plan

No new test files — this is a build change verified by:

```bash
python -m venv /tmp/fresh && /tmp/fresh/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"
/tmp/fresh/bin/python -c "import digitalsreeni_image_annotator as m; print(m.__version__)"
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen timeout 3 /tmp/fresh/bin/digitalsreeni-image-annotator; echo $?   # expect 124
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen timeout 3 /tmp/fresh/bin/sreeni; echo $?                          # expect 124
pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v
pytest tests/ -v
```

Plus a full CI run (the 3-OS × 5-Python matrix is the real verification for the workflow edits).

## Docs to update

- `CLAUDE.md` — Tech Stack ultralytics line (step 5); "Install" quick-reference stays `pip install -e .` but add the `[dev]` variant.
- `README.md` — Development section only (step 6).
- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — ADR-002 (Ultralytics) gains a one-line note recording the reconciled version bound; no new ADR needed for the packaging format itself unless the reviewer asks.
- `TESTING.md` — update any `pip install -r requirements.txt` instructions to `pip install -e ".[dev]"`.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- The package `__init__.py` must stay **Qt-free at import time** (its lazy `__getattr__` exists because `main.py` needs torch imported before Qt loads — ADR-017). The `attr:` dynamic version read imports the package during builds; do not add any eager import to `__init__.py` while touching versioning.
- `sam_trainer.py`'s "verified on ultralytics 8.4.51" docstring is documentation of a verification, not a dependency spec — reconcile the *spec* files, don't rewrite the docstring to match a bound.
- `pip install -e .[dev]` without quotes breaks on some shells (zsh globbing) — use `pip install -e ".[dev]"` everywhere, including CI YAML.
- CI runs on Windows and macOS too: `pytest-xvfb` is Linux-only, hence the environment marker; verify the marker doesn't break `pip install -e ".[dev]"` on Windows runners.
- Do not move `pytest.ini` into `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` in this PR — it works, and mixing config migration into the packaging diff makes review harder. (Fine as a follow-up.)
- Keep the exact entry-point names — `sreeni` is user-facing and documented in `CLAUDE.md`'s Quick Reference.

## Dependencies

None.

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
